 {"id":"brenner_bot-0s7","title":"Legacy: Build excerpt builder component (superseded)","description":"# Task

Create the excerpt builder that allows users to select text from documents and build cited excerpt blocks.

# User Flow

1. User browses a document (e.g., transcript)
2. User selects text
3. Selection popover appears: 'Add to excerpt'
4. Selection added to 'Excerpt Basket' (sidebar or drawer)
5. User can add multiple selections
6. User composes final excerpt with ordering and context
7. Export as formatted markdown for session kickoff

# Excerpt Basket

- List of selections
- Each shows: text snippet, source doc, §-anchor
- Drag to reorder
- Delete button
- 'Add note' for each selection

# Output Format

```markdown
## Context for Brenner Loop

### From §42: The choice of organism
> The choice of the experimental object remains one of the most important things...

### From §87: Abundance trick
> If you can find a protein that dominates...

### User note
[Additional context or question]
```

# Persistence

- Store selections in localStorage
- Clear on 'Start Session' or manual clear

# Integration

- Works within document viewer
- Selections persist across page navigations
- Basket accessible from any page

# Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Text selection triggers popover
- [ ] Selections added to basket
- [ ] Basket shows all selections with metadata
- [ ] Reordering works
- [ ] Export generates correct format

 {"id":"brenner_bot-0xzg","title":"Task: Coverage Threshold Enforcement (80%)","description":"# Goal
Raise test coverage thresholds to 80% and enforce per-file minimum coverage.

# Current State
```typescript
// vitest.config.ts (current)
coverage: {
  thresholds: {
    lines: 50,
    functions: 50,
    branches: 50,
    statements: 50,
  }
}
```

# Target State
```typescript
// vitest.config.ts (target)
coverage: {
  provider: 'v8',
  reporter: ['text', 'lcov', 'html', 'json'],
  reportsDirectory: './coverage',
  include: ['src/**/*.ts', 'src/**/*.tsx'],
  exclude: [
    'src/**/*.test.ts',
    'src/**/*.test.tsx',
    'src/__fixtures__/**',
    'src/types/**',
    '**/*.d.ts',
  ],
  thresholds: {
    // Global thresholds
    lines: 80,
    functions: 80,
    branches: 75,
    statements: 80,
    
    // Per-file enforcement
    perFile: true,
    
    // Critical files with higher thresholds
    'src/lib/artifact-merge.ts': {
      lines: 90,
      functions: 90,
      branches: 85,
    },
    'src/lib/delta-parser.ts': {
      lines: 90,
      functions: 90,
      branches: 85,
    },
  },
  
  // Fail CI on threshold violation
  skipFull: false,
  
  // Check for uncovered lines
  checkCoverage: true,
}
```

# CI Integration
Update GitHub Actions workflow:
```yaml
# .github/workflows/test.yml
- name: Run tests with coverage
  run: bun test --coverage
  
- name: Check coverage thresholds
  run: |
    # Parse coverage report and fail if below threshold
    bun run coverage:check
    
- name: Upload coverage to Codecov
  uses: codecov/codecov-action@v3
  with:
    files: ./apps/web/coverage/lcov.info
    fail_ci_if_error: true
```

# Coverage Badge
Add to README:
```markdown
[![Coverage](https://codecov.io/gh/user/brenner_bot/branch/main/graph/badge.svg)](https://codecov.io/gh/user/brenner_bot)
```

# Gradual Rollout Plan
1. Week 1: Set global to 60%, add perFile
2. Week 2: Raise to 70%, identify low-coverage files
3. Week 3: Raise to 80%, enforce on all files
4. Ongoing: Add per-file exceptions only with justification

# Coverage Exclusion Guidelines
Exclude from coverage (with comment justification):
- Type definitions (*.d.ts)
- Test files and fixtures
- Generated code
- Configuration files
- Development-only code

DO NOT exclude:
- Complex business logic
- Error handling paths
- Edge cases

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Global threshold at 80% lines
- [ ] Per-file enforcement enabled
- [ ] Critical files at 90%
- [ ] CI fails on threshold violation
- [ ] Coverage badge in README
- [ ] Coverage report uploaded to Codecov

 {"id":"brenner_bot-1cv2","title":"Tutorial UX: Mobile Experience & Limitations","description":"# Purpose

Document and handle the reality that tutorial steps requiring terminal commands cannot be fully completed on mobile devices. Ensure mobile users have a good experience anyway.

---

# The Reality

Many tutorial steps require:
- Running terminal commands (git, bun, ./brenner)
- Copying multi-line code blocks
- Running AI agents (Claude Code, Codex)

**These cannot be done on mobile.** A phone or tablet user physically cannot:
- Run a terminal
- Clone a repo
- Execute CLI commands

---

# Design Decisions

## Option A: Gate terminal steps to desktop (CHOSEN)

Show a clear message on terminal-required steps when on mobile:
\`\`\`
📱 This step requires a desktop computer

The commands below need to be run in a terminal on your 
Mac, Windows (WSL), or Linux computer.

You can:
• Read through this step to understand what's involved
• Bookmark this page and return on desktop
• Continue to the next step (some content is mobile-friendly)
\`\`\`

## Option B: Mobile-optimized read-only mode

Automatically switch to "read-only" mode on mobile:
- All content visible
- Code blocks show but note "run on desktop"
- Progress tracking still works
- Can complete "understanding" checkboxes, not "execution" checkboxes

---

# Implementation

## 1. Device Detection
Use user-agent or viewport width to detect mobile.

## 2. Step Metadata
Add \`requiresDesktop: boolean\` to step data:
\`\`\`typescript
interface TutorialStep {
  // ...existing fields
  requiresDesktop?: boolean;
  mobileAlternative?: string; // What to show on mobile instead
}
\`\`\`

## 3. Mobile Message Component
\`\`\`tsx
function MobileDesktopGate({ children, step }: Props) {
  const isMobile = useIsMobile();
  
  if (isMobile && step.requiresDesktop) {
    return <MobileNotice step={step} />;
  }
  
  return children;
}
\`\`\`

## 4. Which Steps Need Desktop?

**Quick Start**:
- Step 2 (Prerequisites): Desktop required
- Step 3 (Clone & Install): Desktop required
- Step 4 (Search Corpus): Desktop required
- Step 5 (Build Excerpt): Desktop required
- Step 6 (First Session): Desktop required
- Step 7 (Understand Output): Mobile OK (reading)

**Agent-Assisted**:
- Steps 2-7: Desktop required
- Step 1, 8: Mobile OK (reading/understanding)

**Multi-Agent**:
- All steps: Desktop required (infrastructure setup)

---

# Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Mobile detection works reliably
- [ ] Desktop-required steps show clear message on mobile
- [ ] Users can still read step content on mobile
- [ ] Progress tracking works on mobile (marks steps as "viewed")
- [ ] Desktop users see no change","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T15:34:28.987529053-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-04T15:08:48.244621638-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-1cv2","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-do3j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T15:35:25.632137409-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-1es5","title":"Spec: Experiment Result Classification and Interpretation","description":"# Goal
Define how experiment results are classified and interpreted into DELTAs.

# Two-Stage Process (Key Insight)

**Stage 1: Classification** (structured, can be assisted)
- What KIND of result is this?
- Pass/fail/error/metrics/observation/anomaly?

**Stage 2: Interpretation** (judgment, guidelines only)
- What does this mean for hypotheses?
- What DELTA should be created?

# Stage 1: Result Classification

## The Taxonomy (Restored)
```typescript
type ExperimentResultType = 
  | "pass"           // Test completed, outcome matches expectation
  | "fail"           // Test completed, outcome contradicts expectation
  | "error"          // Test couldn't complete (crash, timeout, missing deps)
  | "metrics"        // Quantitative measurements without clear pass/fail
  | "observation"    // Qualitative finding requiring interpretation
  | "anomaly";       // Unexpected result that doesn't fit any prediction

interface ClassifiedResult {
  experiment_id: string;
  test_id: string;
  
  // Classification
  result_type: ExperimentResultType;
  confidence: "high" | "medium" | "low";
  
  // Extracted data (type-specific)
  metrics?: Record<string, number>;
  observation?: string;
  error_message?: string;
  
  // Which hypotheses this relates to
  hypothesis_refs: string[];  // ["H1", "H2"]
}
```

## Classification CLI (Assistant, Not Automator)
```bash
# Suggest classification based on output
./brenner.ts experiment classify \
  --session RS-20251230-bio-rrp \
  --experiment exp-a1b2c3

# Output:
# Result type: metrics (confidence: high)
# Extracted metrics:
#   bleu_vanilla: 0.42
#   bleu_rrp: 0.48
#   bleu_freq_penalty: 0.45
# 
# Related hypotheses: H1, H2 (based on test T1 definition)
# 
# Suggested interpretation pattern: "metrics_comparison"
# See: ./brenner.ts experiment interpret --pattern metrics_comparison
```

# Stage 2: Interpretation Patterns

Classification tells you WHAT you have. Interpretation decides WHAT IT MEANS.

## Pattern: pass → Predictions Update
**When**: Test passed, result matches hypothesis prediction
**DELTA**:
```json
{
  "operation": "EDIT",
  "section": "predictions_table",
  "target_id": "P1",
  "payload": {
    "actual_H1": "Confirmed: 0.03 BLEU difference, within equivalence threshold"
  },
  "rationale": "T1 passed: equivalence holds at tested scale"
}
```

## Pattern: fail → Hypothesis Weakening
**When**: Test clearly contradicts a hypothesis
**DELTA**:
```json
{
  "operation": "EDIT",
  "section": "hypothesis_slate",
  "target_id": "H1",
  "payload": {
    "status": "weakened",
    "evidence_against": "T1 showed 15% quality gap at 16k context"
  },
  "rationale": "H1 predicted equivalence; T1 found significant divergence"
}
```

## Pattern: error → Test Blocked
**When**: Experiment couldn't complete
**DELTA**:
```json
{
  "operation": "EDIT",
  "section": "discriminative_tests",
  "target_id": "T1",
  "payload": {
    "status": "blocked",
    "blocker": "OOM at 16k context, need 32GB instance"
  },
  "rationale": "T1 could not complete due to resource constraints"
}
```

## Pattern: metrics → Attach Raw Data
**When**: Have numbers but interpretation unclear
**DELTA**:
```json
{
  "operation": "EDIT",
  "section": "discriminative_tests",
  "target_id": "T1",
  "payload": {
    "actual_results": {
      "bleu_vanilla": 0.42,
      "bleu_rrp": 0.48,
      "bleu_freq_penalty": 0.45
    },
    "status": "awaiting_interpretation"
  },
  "rationale": "T1 produced metrics. Interpretation TBD."
}
```

## Pattern: anomaly → Register for Investigation
**When**: Result doesn't match ANY prediction
**DELTA**:
```json
{
  "operation": "ADD",
  "section": "anomaly_register",
  "payload": {
    "name": "Latency spike at 4k boundary",
    "observation": "RRP showed 3x latency at exactly 4096 tokens",
    "conflicts_with": ["H1", "H2"],
    "status": "active"
  },
  "rationale": "No hypothesis predicted latency effects"
}
```

## Pattern: observation → Critique or Assumption
**When**: Qualitative insight from running experiment
**DELTA** (if it challenges framing):
```json
{
  "operation": "ADD",
  "section": "adversarial_critique",
  "payload": {
    "name": "Metric choice confound",
    "attack": "BLEU may not capture the coherence benefits RRP provides",
    "evidence": "Running T1 revealed outputs differed in structure, not token overlap",
    "current_status": "Observed during experiment, needs investigation"
  },
  "rationale": "T1 execution revealed potential metric confound"
}
```

# When Patterns Don't Fit
- If result is ambiguous between patterns → discuss in thread
- If multiple patterns apply → create multiple DELTAs
- If no pattern fits → add to anomaly_register with status: "unclassified"

# CLI Helper
```bash
# Show interpretation guidance for a pattern
./brenner.ts experiment interpret --pattern anomaly

# Interactive mode: walk through classification and interpretation
./brenner.ts experiment interpret --interactive \
  --session RS-20251230-bio-rrp \
  --experiment exp-a1b2c3
```

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] ExperimentResultType taxonomy defined
- [ ] ClassifiedResult schema defined
- [ ] experiment classify command implemented
- [ ] All interpretation patterns documented in specs/
- [ ] experiment interpret helper command
- [ ] Guidance on when to ask for help

# Relationship to Other Work
- Depends on: Execution protocol (brenner_bot-t62l)
- Uses: Existing delta-parser and artifact-merge
- Enables: Artifact evolution from experiment evidence","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-30T18:51:12.453215707-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-31T00:21:04.888114637-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-31T00:21:04.888114637-05:00","close_reason":"All 4 child tasks completed: ExperimentResult schema (1es5.1), artifact types (1es5.2), experiment encode CLI (1es5.3), golden fixtures + tests (1es5.4)","labels":["infrastructure","protocol","research-loop"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-1es5","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-t62l","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T18:52:25.940633427-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-1es5","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.12","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T18:59:54.950352935-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}

 {"id":"brenner_bot-1rbd","title":"Task: Search Workflow E2E Tests","description":"# Goal
Create comprehensive E2E tests for the search workflow - the MOST CRITICAL user journey that is currently UNTESTED.

# User Journey to Test

## Happy Path
1. User lands on any page
2. Opens spotlight search (Cmd+K / Ctrl+K)
3. Types search query ("brenner", "§103", "third alternative")
4. Sees results grouped by category (sections, quotes, documents)
5. Uses keyboard to navigate results (↑↓)
6. Presses Enter to select result
7. Is navigated to correct document/section
8. Section is highlighted/focused
9. Can continue searching from new location

## Edge Cases
- Empty search query
- No results found
- Very long search query
- Special characters in query
- Search while results loading (debounce)
- Rapid typing (debounce stress)
- Search on mobile (touch interactions)
- Search with slow network
- Search after navigation

# Test File Structure
```typescript
// e2e/search-workflow.spec.ts
import { test, expect } from './utils/test-fixtures';

test.describe('Search Workflow', () => {
  test.describe('Desktop', () => {
    test('opens spotlight with Cmd+K and focuses input', async ({ page, logger }) => {
      await navigateTo(page, logger, '/corpus');
      
      await withStep(logger, page, 'Open spotlight search', async () => {
        await page.keyboard.press('Meta+k');
      });
      
      await withStep(logger, page, 'Verify search input focused', async () => {
        await expect(page.getByRole('searchbox')).toBeFocused();
      });
      
      logger.info('Search opened successfully');
    });
    
    test('searches and navigates to section result', async ({ page, logger }) => {
      await navigateTo(page, logger, '/corpus');
      await page.keyboard.press('Meta+k');
      
      await withStep(logger, page, 'Type search query', async () => {
        await page.getByRole('searchbox').fill('§103');
        logger.debug('Typed search query: §103');
      });
      
      await withStep(logger, page, 'Wait for results', async () => {
        await page.waitForSelector('[data-testid="search-result"]', { timeout: 2000 });
        const resultCount = await page.locator('[data-testid="search-result"]').count();
        logger.info(`Found ${resultCount} search results`);
      });
      
      await withStep(logger, page, 'Select first result with keyboard', async () => {
        await page.keyboard.press('ArrowDown');
        await page.keyboard.press('Enter');
      });
      
      await withStep(logger, page, 'Verify navigation to section', async () => {
        await expect(page).toHaveURL(/.*#.*103.*/);
        // Or verify section is highlighted
        await expect(page.locator('[data-section="103"]')).toBeVisible();
      });
    });
    
    test('handles no results gracefully', async ({ page, logger }) => {
      await navigateTo(page, logger, '/corpus');
      await page.keyboard.press('Meta+k');
      
      await page.getByRole('searchbox').fill('xyznonexistent123');
      
      await withStep(logger, page, 'Verify no results message', async () => {
        await expect(page.getByText(/no results/i)).toBeVisible();
      });
    });
    
    test('closes on Escape key', async ({ page, logger }) => {
      await navigateTo(page, logger, '/corpus');
      await page.keyboard.press('Meta+k');
      await expect(page.getByRole('searchbox')).toBeVisible();
      
      await page.keyboard.press('Escape');
      await expect(page.getByRole('searchbox')).not.toBeVisible();
    });
  });
  
  test.describe('Mobile', () => {
    test.use({ viewport: { width: 375, height: 667 } });
    
    test('opens search via touch on mobile', async ({ page, logger }) => {
      await navigateTo(page, logger, '/corpus');
      
      await withStep(logger, page, 'Tap search icon', async () => {
        await page.getByRole('button', { name: /search/i }).tap();
      });
      
      await expect(page.getByRole('searchbox')).toBeVisible();
    });
  });
});
```

 {"id":"brenner_bot-1uu","title":"TanStack Foundation: Package Installation & Provider Setup","description":"# Goal
Install all TanStack packages and configure the React provider tree. This is the foundation that enables all other TanStack work.

## Why Priority 0?
This task blocks ALL other TanStack integration work. Without providers configured, no hooks work.

## Packages to Install
- @tanstack/react-query (server state, caching)
- @tanstack/react-virtual (list virtualization)
- @tanstack/react-table (table management)
- @tanstack/react-form + @tanstack/zod-form-adapter (form handling)
- @tanstack/react-store (client state)
- @tanstack/react-query-devtools (development only)

## Implementation Steps

### 1. Install packages
```bash
cd apps/web
bun add @tanstack/react-query @tanstack/react-virtual @tanstack/react-table @tanstack/react-form @tanstack/zod-form-adapter @tanstack/react-store
bun add -D @tanstack/react-query-devtools
```

### 2. Create QueryClient provider
Create `apps/web/src/components/providers/QueryProvider.tsx`:
- Configure staleTime (5 minutes for corpus data - it rarely changes)
- Configure gcTime (30 minutes - keep parsed documents in memory)
- Enable devtools in development

### 3. Update root layout
Wrap app in providers in `apps/web/src/app/layout.tsx`:
- QueryClientProvider must wrap entire app
- Use 'use client' boundary appropriately

### 4. Configure SSR/hydration
- Set up prefetching for corpus data on server
- Handle hydration to avoid flash of loading state

## Considerations

### Server Components Boundary
Next.js 16 uses Server Components by default. TanStack providers are client components.
Solution: Create a `Providers.tsx` client component that wraps children.

### Bundle Size
All packages together: ~40KB gzipped. Acceptable given we remove ~500 lines of custom code.

### DevTools
Only include in development builds. Use dynamic import with `next/dynamic`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All packages installed and importable
- [ ] QueryClientProvider wraps the app
- [ ] DevTools visible in development
- [ ] Build succeeds without errors
- [ ] No hydration warnings in console

## Files to Create/Modify
- apps/web/package.json (dependencies)
- apps/web/src/components/providers/QueryProvider.tsx (new)
- apps/web/src/components/providers/index.tsx (new - re-export)
- apps/web/src/app/layout.tsx (wrap with providers)","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T23:14:55.611825547-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T23:26:42.293574531-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-29T23:26:42.293574531-05:00","close_reason":"TanStack foundation complete: packages installed, QueryProvider configured with SSR-safe client, layout wrapped with providers","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-1uu","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-70b","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T23:15:01.701894974-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-1v26","title":"EPIC: Infrastructure & API - Persistence, Real-Time, Collaboration Foundation","description":"# Infrastructure & API - Persistence, Real-Time, Collaboration Foundation

## Purpose
Build the technical foundation that enables all other features: data persistence, real-time updates, and the groundwork for future collaboration features.

## Components

### 1. Session Persistence Layer
Sessions need to persist across:
- Browser refreshes
- Device switches (future)
- Long time gaps

**Options**:
- **LocalStorage/IndexedDB**: Simple, works offline, no backend
- **Supabase/Postgres**: Enables sync, collaboration, analytics
- **Git-backed**: Agent Mail style, fully auditable

**Recommendation**: Start with localStorage, add Supabase later for cross-device sync.

```typescript
interface SessionStorage {
  save(session: Session): Promise<void>;
  load(sessionId: string): Promise<Session | null>;
  list(): Promise<SessionSummary[]>;
  delete(sessionId: string): Promise<void>;
  
  // Future
  sync?(userId: string): Promise<void>;
  share?(sessionId: string): Promise<ShareLink>;
}
```

### 2. Real-Time Updates (Agent Responses)
When agents are working, show progress:
- Agent status changes
- Partial responses (streaming)
- Completion notifications

**Via Agent Mail**:
- Poll inbox periodically
- Show new messages as they arrive
- WebSocket connection if available

**UI Implications**:
- Status indicators update live
- Response panels populate progressively
- Users see when agents are working

### 3. Hypothesis Versioning
Git-style versioning for hypotheses:
```typescript
interface HypothesisCommit {
  id: string;
  parentId?: string;
  hypothesis: HypothesisCard;
  message: string;
  timestamp: Date;
  trigger: 'manual' | 'operator' | 'evidence' | 'agent';
}
```

This enables:
- Full history navigation
- Diff between versions
- Branching (explore alternatives)
- Merging (combine insights)

### 4. Export/Import
Enable moving sessions between devices/users:
```typescript
interface SessionExport {
  format: 'json' | 'markdown';
  version: string;
  session: Session;
  artifacts: Artifact[];
  createdAt: Date;
  checksum: string;
}

function exportSession(session: Session): SessionExport;
function importSession(data: SessionExport): Session;
```

### 5. Future API Foundation
For v2, we'll want:
- REST API for session CRUD
- WebSocket for real-time
- Public API for integrations

Design storage now with this in mind:
- Stable IDs
- Clean separation of concerns
- Serializable state

### 6. Analytics Integration
Connect to GA4 tracking:
- Session events (started, completed, abandoned)
- Operator usage
- Agent dispatch patterns
- Time spent per phase

This informs:
- Which features are actually used
- Where users get stuck
- What drives completion

## Security Considerations
- Sessions may contain sensitive hypotheses
- Encrypt at rest if stored remotely
- No PII in analytics
- Clear data retention policies

## Performance
- Session state should load instantly
- Large sessions shouldn't slow UI
- Pagination for long evidence ledgers
- Lazy load agent responses

## Dependencies
- None (this is foundational infrastructure)

## Success Criteria
- Sessions persist reliably
- Real-time updates work
- Export/import round-trips cleanly
- Foundation supports future collaboration

```typescript
interface Session {
  id: string;
  createdAt: Date;
  updatedAt: Date;
  phase: SessionPhase;

  hypothesisHistory: HypothesisVersion[];

    scaleCheck?: ScaleCheckResult;
  };
  
  agentResponses: AgentResponse[];
  synthesis?: SynthesisResult;
  
  evidenceLedger: EvidenceEntry[];
  artifacts: ResearchArtifact[];
  
  // Git-style versioning
  commits: SessionCommit[];
}
```

### SessionPhase
```typescript
type SessionPhase =
  | 'intake'
  | 'sharpening'
  | 'level_split'
  | 'exclusion_test'
  | 'object_transpose'
  | 'scale_check'
  | 'agent_dispatch'
  | 'synthesis'
  | 'evidence_gathering'
  | 'revision'
  | 'complete';
```

### HypothesisCard, EvidenceEntry, etc.
Define all the nested types from the epic descriptions.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All core interfaces defined with JSDoc comments

- Must be accessible (not color-only)

- Scroll matched section into view
- Temporary highlight effect (fade after 2-3 seconds)

 {"id":"brenner_bot-v2e","title":"Build native React document renderer with custom components","description":"# Task

Build a rich document renderer using NATIVE React/TypeScript components that fully leverage Tailwind and our design system. NOT a generic markdown viewer!

# Philosophy

The content deserves custom treatment:
- Brenner quotes should look *amazing* (not just gray italic blocks)
- Operator symbols (⊘, 𝓛, ≡, ✂) should be styled distinctively
- Code blocks should use our OKLCH colors
- Tables should be interactive, not HTML tables
- § anchors should be beautiful and functional

# Architecture

```
Markdown source → MDX/AST parser → Custom React components
```

1. Parse markdown at build time (or server-side)
2. Map each AST node to a custom component
3. Each component uses our design system

# Custom Components Needed

## Typography
- `<Heading level={1|2|3|4} id='anchor'>...</Heading>`
- `<Paragraph>...</Paragraph>`
- `<Lead>...</Lead>` (intro paragraphs)

## Brenner-specific
- `<BrennerQuote author='Brenner' source='§42'>...</BrennerQuote>` - Special quote styling with gradient border, attribution
- `<OperatorSymbol symbol='⊘' name='Level-split' />` - Styled operator with tooltip
- `<SectionAnchor id='§42' />` - Animated anchor with copy functionality

## Content blocks
- `<CodeBlock language='typescript'>...</CodeBlock>` - Syntax highlighting with our colors
- `<Table columns={[...]} data={[...]} />` - Interactive table with sorting, hover
- `<Callout type='insight|warning|note'>...</Callout>` - Styled callout boxes
- `<Definition term='Gedanken organism'>...</Definition>` - Glossary-style definitions

## Interactive
- `<CopyButton text='...' />` - Copy citation/text
- `<ShareButton quote='...' />` - Share to social/clipboard
- `<CollapsibleSection title='...'>...</CollapsibleSection>`

# Implementation

Use `next-mdx-remote` or `@mdx-js/mdx` to:
1. Parse markdown to AST
2. Provide custom components mapping
3. Render with our components

```tsx
import { MDXRemote } from 'next-mdx-remote/rsc';

const components = {
  h1: (props) => <Heading level={1} {...props} />,
  blockquote: (props) => <BrennerQuote {...props} />,
  // ...
};

<MDXRemote source={content} components={components} />
```

# Quote Styling Example

```tsx
function BrennerQuote({ children, source }) {
  return (
    <motion.blockquote
      className="relative my-8 border-l-4 border-primary/50 bg-card/50 p-6 
                 rounded-r-xl italic text-lg leading-relaxed"
      initial={{ opacity: 0, x: -20 }}
      whileInView={{ opacity: 1, x: 0 }}
    >
      <span className="absolute -left-3 -top-3 text-4xl text-primary/30">"</span>
      {children}
      {source && (
        <cite className="mt-4 block text-sm text-muted-foreground not-italic">
          — Sydney Brenner, {source}
        </cite>
      )}
    </motion.blockquote>
  );
}
```

# TOC Generation

Still generate TOC from headings, but render with our components:
- Sticky sidebar on desktop
- Collapsible on mobile  
- Scroll-spy highlighting
- Smooth scroll on click

# Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All markdown elements map to custom components
- [ ] Typography uses our design system
- [ ] Quotes look beautiful (not generic)
- [ ] Operators have distinctive styling
- [ ] § anchors are interactive
- [ ] TOC works with scroll-spy
- [ ] Animations respect reduced motion
- [ ] Responsive layout","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:14:46.390974262-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T19:29:11.455541735-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-29T19:29:11.455541735-05:00","close_reason":"Superseded by brenner_bot-5so.4.1.1 + brenner_bot-5so.4.1.2","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-v2e","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-8yn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:16:24.931287472-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-v2e","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-y6z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:23:06.908825369-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-v2e","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-wpb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:23:06.941047723-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}

## Objective
Implement persistent logging of all state transitions for audit trail and history queries.

## Implementation Details

### File Location
`apps/web/src/lib/hypothesis-history.ts`

### Storage Structure
```
.research/
└── transitions/
    ├── RS-20251230-transitions.json  # Per-session
    └── ...
```

### File Format
```json
{
  "sessionId": "RS-20251230",
  "transitions": [
    {
      "hypothesisId": "H-RS20251230-001",
      "fromState": "proposed",
      "toState": "active",
      "trigger": "activate",
      "triggeredBy": "user",
      "timestamp": "2025-12-30T10:05:00Z"
    },
    {
      "hypothesisId": "H-RS20251230-001",
      "fromState": "active",
      "toState": "killed",
      "trigger": "kill",
      "triggeredBy": "T-RS20251230-002",
      "reason": "ATAC-seq showed open chromatin, contradicting methylation-only hypothesis",
      "timestamp": "2025-12-31T15:30:00Z"
    }
  ]
}
```

### Core Functions
```typescript
export async function logTransition(transition: StateTransition): Promise<void>

export async function getHypothesisHistory(hypothesisId: string): Promise<StateTransition[]>

export async function getSessionTransitions(sessionId: string): Promise<StateTransition[]>

export async function getTransitionsInRange(
  start: Date,
  end: Date
): Promise<StateTransition[]>

export interface HypothesisTimeline {
  hypothesisId: string;
  statement: string;
  transitions: Array<{
    fromState: HypothesisState;
    toState: HypothesisState;
    trigger: TransitionTrigger;
    timestamp: string;
    triggeredBy?: string;
  }>;
  currentState: HypothesisState;
}

export async function buildHypothesisTimeline(
  hypothesisId: string
): Promise<HypothesisTimeline>
```

### Query Examples
```typescript
// "What killed hypothesis H1?"
const history = await getHypothesisHistory('H-RS20251230-001');
const killTransition = history.find(t => t.toState === 'killed');
// → T-RS20251230-002 killed it at 2025-12-31T15:30:00Z

// "What happened in session RS-20251230?"
const transitions = await getSessionTransitions('RS-20251230');
// → [activate H1, activate H2, kill H1, ...]
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All transitions logged to per-session files
- [ ] Query by hypothesis ID
- [ ] Query by session ID
- [ ] Query by time range
- [ ] Timeline reconstruction shows full history
- [ ] Append-only log (no editing history)

## Dependencies
- Task: Define State Transition Types

 {"id":"brenner_bot-voa","title":"TanStack Query: Migrate Document Viewers to Query Hooks","description":"# Goal
Migrate TranscriptViewer and DistillationViewer to use the new Query hooks instead of receiving data as props from Server Components.

## Background
Currently, document viewers receive pre-fetched data as props:
```typescript
// TranscriptViewer.tsx
interface TranscriptViewerProps {
  data: TranscriptData;  // Passed from Server Component
}
```

This works but means:
1. Data is re-fetched on every navigation
2. No caching between page visits
3. Server must parse on every request

## Migration Strategy

### Option A: Keep Server Fetch + Hydrate (Recommended)
Server Component prefetches, passes to client via HydrationBoundary.
- Best for SEO (content in initial HTML)
- Best for performance (no client-side loading flash)
- Query still provides caching for subsequent navigations

### Option B: Full Client-Side Fetch
Remove server prefetch, let Query handle everything.
- Simpler code
- Shows loading state on first visit
- Worse SEO

**We choose Option A** because the corpus pages are content-heavy and benefit from SSR.

## Implementation

### 1. Update corpus/[doc]/page.tsx
```typescript
import { HydrationBoundary, QueryClient, dehydrate } from '@tanstack/react-query';

export default async function CorpusDocPage({ params }) {
  const queryClient = new QueryClient();
  
  await queryClient.prefetchQuery({
    queryKey: ['corpus', 'doc', params.doc],
    queryFn: () => readCorpusDoc(params.doc),
  });

  return (
    <HydrationBoundary state={dehydrate(queryClient)}>
      <DocumentViewer docId={params.doc} />
    </HydrationBoundary>
  );
}
```

### 2. Update TranscriptViewer
```typescript
export function TranscriptViewer({ docId }: { docId: string }) {
  const { data, isLoading, error } = useCorpusDoc(docId);
  
  if (isLoading) return <TranscriptSkeleton />;
  if (error) return <ErrorBoundary error={error} />;
  
  // ... rest of component
}
```

### 3. Update DistillationViewer similarly

## Considerations

### Skeleton Components
Create loading skeletons that match the layout:
- TranscriptSkeleton: TOC + content placeholders
- DistillationSkeleton: Header + section placeholders

### Error Boundaries
Create error UI for:
- Document not found
- Parse failure
- Network error

### Backward Compatibility
During migration, support both patterns:
- Accept data as prop (old pattern)
- Use Query if docId provided (new pattern)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] TranscriptViewer uses Query hook
- [ ] DistillationViewer uses Query hook
- [ ] SSR still works (content in initial HTML)
- [ ] Navigation between docs is instant (cached)
- [ ] Loading states are graceful
- [ ] Error states are handled

## Files to Modify
- apps/web/src/app/corpus/[doc]/page.tsx
- apps/web/src/components/transcript/TranscriptViewer.tsx
- apps/web/src/components/distillation/DistillationViewer.tsx

## Files to Create
- apps/web/src/components/transcript/TranscriptSkeleton.tsx
- apps/web/src/components/distillation/DistillationSkeleton.tsx","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T23:15:54.657062985-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T23:48:57.870114122-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-29T23:48:57.870114122-05:00","close_reason":"Migrated document viewers to TanStack Query hooks with HydrationBoundary for SSR prefetching and navigation caching.  {"id":"brenner_bot-xlk2.7","title":"FEATURE: Agent Debate Mode - Multi-Round Adversarial Dialogue","description":"# Agent Debate Mode - Multi-Round Adversarial Dialogue

## The Gap This Fills
Current design: Dispatch all agents → Wait → Collect responses → Synthesize

But this misses the value of DEBATE:
- Devil's Advocate challenges Experiment Designer's protocol
- Brenner Channeler responds to Devil's Advocate's objection
- Back-and-forth sharpens the analysis

## Feature Design

### Debate Structure
```typescript
interface AgentDebate {
  id: string;
  sessionId: string;
  topic: string;  // What's being debated
  
  // Participants
  participants: AgentRole[];
  moderator: 'system' | AgentRole;
  
  // Rounds
  rounds: DebateRound[];
  maxRounds: number;
  currentRound: number;
  
  // Outcome
  status: 'in_progress' | 'concluded' | 'timed_out';
  conclusion?: DebateConclusion;
}

interface DebateRound {
  number: number;
  speaker: AgentRole;
  content: string;
  replyingTo?: string;  // Which previous statement
  
  // Analysis
  newPointsMade: string[];
  objectionsRaised: string[];
  concessionsGiven: string[];
}

interface DebateConclusion {
  consensus: string[];       // What all agents agreed on
  unresolved: string[];      // Genuine disagreements
  keyInsight: string;        // Most important takeaway
  winningArguments: string[];// Strongest points made
}
```

### Key Features

#### 1. Structured Debate Formats

**Format A: Oxford-Style**
- Motion: "This hypothesis is falsifiable"
- Proposition: Experiment Designer argues for
- Opposition: Devil's Advocate argues against
- Judge: Brenner Channeler

**Format B: Socratic Questioning**
- Brenner Channeler asks probing questions
- Other agents must respond
- Reveals weak points through dialogue

**Format C: Steel-Manning Contest**
- Each agent must steelman the hypothesis
- Then each agent tries to defeat their own steelman
- Best attack wins

#### 2. Debate Viewer
```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  AGENT DEBATE: Is this hypothesis falsifiable?              │
│  Round 3 of 5                                               │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                             │
│  🟢 Experiment Designer (Round 1):                          │
│  "I propose a three-arm RCT that would definitively test..." │
│                                                             │
│  🔴 Devil's Advocate (Round 2):                             │
│  "Your RCT has a fatal flaw: you can't blind participants   │
│   to which arm they're in. They'll know if they're getting  │
│   the algorithmic feed or not. Demand characteristics       │
│   invalidate the design."                                   │
│                                                             │
│  🟢 Experiment Designer (Round 3):                          │
│  "Fair point. I modify my proposal: instead of removing     │
│   the algorithm, we modify it subtly. Participants won't    │
│   know which variant they're receiving..."                  │
│                                                             │
│  ⏳ Waiting for 🔴 Devil's Advocate...                       │
│                                                             │
│  [Pause Debate] [Skip to Conclusion] [Inject Question]      │
│                                                             │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

#### 3. User Injection
User can inject questions or challenges mid-debate:
- "Devil's Advocate, what about selection bias?"
- Forces the debate to address specific concerns

#### 4. Debate Outcomes
After debate concludes:
- Extract all objections to Objection Register
- Extract all experimental designs
- Identify where debate resolved disagreement
- Flag persistent disagreements

### Integration Points
- Agent Tribunal → Enhanced with debate option
- Objection Register → Populated from debate
- Test Queue → Experimental designs added

## Why This is Critical
Single-round responses are shallow. Debate:
- Sharpens arguments through opposition
- Reveals weaknesses in real-time
- Produces refined experimental designs
- More closely mirrors how good research happens

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Multiple debate formats available
- [ ] Multi-round exchanges work
- [ ] User can inject questions
- [ ] Conclusions properly extracted
- [ ] Debate transcript preserved

 {"id":"brenner_bot-y6z","title":"Create BrennerQuote component with beautiful styling","description":"# Task

Create a stunning quote component specifically for Brenner quotes.

# Why Special Treatment

Brenner's quotes are the crown jewels of this project. They deserve more than gray italic text. Each quote should feel like wisdom worth savoring.

# Design

```
┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  ❝                                                      │
│    Exclusion is always a tremendously good              │
│    thing in science.                                    │
│                                                         │
│                           — Sydney Brenner, §42         │
│                                      [📋] [🔗]          │
└────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

# Visual Elements

1. **Opening quote mark** - Large, decorative, in primary color (faded)
2. **Quote text** - Larger font, good line height, slightly italic
3. **Gradient border** - Left border with primary→accent gradient
4. **Background** - Subtle card background with glassmorphism
5. **Attribution** - Right-aligned, smaller, with § link
6. **Action buttons** - Copy, Share (appear on hover)

# Animation

- Fade + slide in on scroll reveal
- Subtle glow on hover
- Button reveal on hover

# Props

```tsx
interface BrennerQuoteProps {
  children: React.ReactNode;
  source?: string;        // e.g., '§42' or 'Interview, 1998'
  sourceUrl?: string;     // Link to transcript section
  variant?: 'default' | 'featured' | 'inline';
}
```

# Variants

- **default** - Standard quote block
- **featured** - Larger, more prominent (for landing page)
- **inline** - Compact, for use within paragraphs

# Accessibility

- Proper `<blockquote>` semantics
- `<cite>` for attribution
- Focus states on buttons
- Screen reader announces as quote

# Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Quote renders with all visual elements
- [ ] Animation on scroll reveal
- [ ] Copy button works
- [ ] Share button opens share dialog
- [ ] § link navigates correctly
- [ ] All three variants work
- [ ] Accessible markup","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:21:53.472975562-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T19:29:11.423890236-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-29T19:29:11.423890236-05:00","close_reason":"Folded into landing quote presentation (brenner_bot-9o2)","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-y6z","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-8yn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:23:06.812013867-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-y6z","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-2n1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:23:06.847436402-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}

 {"id":"brenner_bot-y8px","title":"TASK: Build Hypothesis Comparison View","description":"
## Side-by-Side Hypothesis Comparison

When users have competing hypotheses (via Competing Hypotheses Arena - an1n.6), they need a clear way to compare them.

### Comparison View Design

\`\`\`
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Compare Hypotheses                              [Exit Compare] │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                 │
│  Hypothesis A                    vs    Hypothesis B            │
│  ────────────────                      ────────────────         │
│                                                                 │
│  Statement:                            Statement:               │
│  "Social media causes                  "Pre-existing anxiety    │
│  anxiety through                       leads to increased       │
│  comparison mechanisms"                social media use"        │
│                                                                 │
│  Mechanism:                            Mechanism:               │
│  Upward social comparison              Anxiety → seeking        │
│  triggers inadequacy                   reassurance online       │
│                                                                 │
│  Key Prediction:                       Key Prediction:          │
│  Reducing SM time                      Treating anxiety will    │
│  decreases anxiety                     reduce SM usage          │
│                                                                 │
│  Confidence: 72%                       Confidence: 45%          │
│  ████████████░░░░                      ████████░░░░░░░░         │
│                                                                 │
│  ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── │
│                                                                 │
│  Discriminating Tests:                                          │
│  • Algorithm change → A predicts decrease, B predicts no change │
│  • Clinical treatment → A predicts no change, B predicts effect │
│                                                                 │
│  [Design New Test] [Resolve Competition]                        │
│                                                                 │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
\`\`\`

### Comparison Features

#### 1. Field-by-Field Diff

Highlight differences:
\`\`\`typescript
interface ComparisonResult {
  field: keyof HypothesisCard;
  valueA: string;
  valueB: string;
  similarity: number;  // 0-1
  isConflicting: boolean;
}
\`\`\`

#### 2. Prediction Conflict Matrix

Show where hypotheses make DIFFERENT predictions:
\`\`\`
                    Hypothesis A    Hypothesis B
Test 1 result       supports        challenges
Test 2 result       challenges      supports      ← DISCRIMINATING
Test 3 result       inconclusive    inconclusive
\`\`\`

#### 3. Evidence Summary

Which tests favor which hypothesis:
\`\`\`
Evidence so far:
• 2 tests favor Hypothesis A
• 1 test favors Hypothesis B  
• 1 test inconclusive

Current leader: Hypothesis A (67%)
\`\`\`

#### 4. Resolution Actions

When competition is resolved:
- Declare winner
- Merge insights from loser
- Archive loser (with learning notes)
- Generate comparison section in Research Brief

### Entry Points

1. From Competing Hypotheses Arena
2. From session dashboard (when multiple hypos)
3. From "Compare" button on hypothesis card

### File Locations
\`\`\`
apps/web/src/components/brenner-loop/ComparisonView.tsx
apps/web/src/components/brenner-loop/HypothesisDiff.tsx
apps/web/src/components/brenner-loop/PredictionMatrix.tsx
apps/web/src/lib/brenner-loop/comparison.ts
\`\`\`

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Side-by-side view works
- [ ] Differences highlighted
- [ ] Prediction conflicts shown
- [ ] Evidence summary displayed
- [ ] Resolution flow works

### Depends On
- an1n.6 (Competing Hypotheses Arena)
- an1n.3 (HypothesisCard Component)
